title: Acmella poliolepidica
taxonomy: Acmella poliolepidica is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Acmella, which consists of several species of plants often referred to as 'buzzing' or 'licking' plants due to their unique ability to produce a tingling sensation when touched. Acmella poliolepidica is a relatively unknown species within the genus Acmella. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Acmella poliolepidica, also known as toothache plant, is a species of flowering plant in the dill family (Apiaceae) native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from toothache pain, headaches, and other minor aches and pains, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine. The bioactive constituents of Acmella poliolepidica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acmella poliolepidica is often made into teas, infusions, or poultices, and is also used as a tincture or extract in modern herbal preparations.
